# Hub-and-Spoke Authority

## The Physics

In any multi-entity organization (venture studio, holding company, franchise),
the hub entity has visibility across all spoke entities, but spoke entities
see only their own scope. A venture studio CEO sees all ventures. Each venture
CEO sees only their venture. This is not a restriction — it is organizational
physics that prevents information overload while maintaining strategic oversight.

The hub can share insights derived from cross-spoke patterns ("I've seen this
before in another context") without exposing raw spoke data to other spokes.

## Why It Matters

- **Strategic oversight**: The hub sees patterns across all spokes.
- **Data isolation**: Spokes cannot leak data to each other.
- **Scalability**: Adding a new spoke doesn't change hub governance or other spokes.
- **Trust boundaries**: Each spoke trusts the hub but not necessarily other spokes.

## Academic/Industry Backing

- Venture studio organizational models (Foundry, Idealab, Betaworks).
- Hub-and-spoke airline networks: efficiency through centralized coordination.
- Multi-tenancy in SaaS: tenant isolation with platform-level analytics.

## Connected Objects

- JOS-C03 (Boundary Management) — hub-and-spoke IS boundary management at org level
- JOS-P02 (Data Boundary Integrity) — data isolation between spokes
